L1_COMPLETING_WHOLE.title: Chapter 1: Completing the Whole
coreIdeas: Make 10 first, then finish the sum.
Use visual movement (circle or number hops) before symbolic steps.
Pause every 45-60 seconds for a student checkpoint.

teachingScript.teacherLine: First we learn the big idea: make a friendly base before solving.
teachingScript.boardAction: Write friendly base 10 and show one simple make-10 example.
teachingScript.microPractice: Say one sentence on what base-10 completion means.

teachingScript.teacherLine: Now I will draw the ten-point circle and we move clockwise together.
teachingScript.boardAction: Draw circle labels 10..1 and animate a jump path.
teachingScript.checkpointPrompt: Start at 3 and move 4 clockwise. Where do you land?
teachingScript.microPractice: Try one more jump with your own numbers.

teachingScript.teacherLine: We will do one more circle movement and then you will answer.
teachingScript.boardAction: Highlight start point and trace jump arrows slowly.
teachingScript.checkpointPrompt: From 5, jump 3 clockwise. Where do you land?
teachingScript.microPractice: Try two circle jumps without writing.

teachingScript.teacherLine: Let us find what is missing to reach 10 using bars.
teachingScript.boardAction: Draw a 10-bar and shade the missing part.
teachingScript.checkpointPrompt: What is the deficiency of 7 from 10?

teachingScript.teacherLine: We will split the second number to complete 10 first.
teachingScript.checkpointPrompt: How do you solve 9 + 6 using the same method?
teachingScript.microPractice: Solve 7 + 8 and say each step aloud.

teachingScript.teacherLine: I will break subtraction into easy jumps on a number line.
teachingScript.checkpointPrompt: Can you do 16-7 with the same jumps?
